shortAnswer: To apply for NIH SBIR Biotech Grants 2026-2027, start by reviewing the eligibility criteria and preparing a project proposal. Complete 2026-2027 guide to NIH SBIR/STTR grants for biotech startups. Phase I up to $285K, Phase II up to $2M for therapeutics, medical devices, diagnostics, digital health innovation. Funding available: up to $285K (with related programs offering $2M).
shortAnswerQuestion: How can my business apply for NIH SBIR Biotech Grants 2026-2027 in 2026?

faq.question: Does the NIH only fund medical devices?
faq.answer: No. The NIH funds a broad spectrum of health-related innovations, including digital health apps, therapeutics, diagnostics, research tools, and behavioral interventions.

faq.question: What is the difference between NIH Phase I and Phase II?
faq.answer: Phase I (up to $295,000) is strictly for proving the scientific feasibility of your concept. Phase II (up to $1.97M) is for continued R&D, prototype development, and clinical evaluation.

faq.question: Can academic researchers apply?
faq.answer: Academic researchers can participate, but the applicant organization must be a for-profit small business (under 500 employees). STTR grants require formal partnership with a non-profit research institution.

expertTip.title: Scientific Rigor
expertTip.content: NIH reviewers are PhD scientists. Your proposal must demonstrate rigorous scientific methodology—not just business potential.
